name: Signet Proof Workshop
description: Proof derivation engine operating on cached credentials. Implements SD-JWT selective disclosure presentations, BBS+ unlinkable proofs (fresh internally-generated nonce per presentation for unlinkability), Bulletproof range proofs from Pedersen commitments using dalek bulletproofs (nightly). All proofs are domain-bound (relying party origin/DID + timestamp/nonce) and time-limited. Proof composition combines multiple heterogeneous proofs into a single composite presentation. Typestate pipeline (ProofRequest → ProofPlan → ProofBundle → BoundPresentation) enforces correct sequencing at compile time. An AuditManifest is co-produced with every BoundPresentation to satisfy the audit chain invariant. Performance SLAs: <20ms for SD-JWT and BBS+ derivation, <200ms for Bulletproof range proofs. BBS+ unlinkability invariant is statistically tested (N≥1000, chi-squared + K-S). Serialization: deterministic CBOR (ciborium) for composite presentations, SD-JWT compact serialization for its component. Security: Zeroizing<> wrappers on all secret material, RevealedClaims newtype prevents accidental full-credential leakage, Debug impls redact secrets, no algorithm negotiation, pipeline timeout = deny (fail-secure).

types.name: RpIdentifier
types.description: Relying party identifier — either a web origin URL or a DID. Covers both web-based and decentralized identity-based verifiers.

types.name: Nonce

fields.name: value
fields.description: 32 bytes of cryptographically random nonce data. Wrapped in Zeroizing<> in implementation.

types.description: Cryptographic nonce — 32 bytes of random data. Used for challenge-response freshness and domain binding. Constant-time equality comparison. Debug impl redacts value.

types.name: Timestamp

fields.name: seconds_since_epoch
fields.description: Seconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01T00:00:00Z). Must be positive.

validators.error_message: Timestamp must be a positive Unix epoch value

types.description: Unix timestamp in seconds (UTC). Used for proof issuance and expiry times.

types.name: DomainBinding

fields.name: rp_identifier
fields.description: The relying party this proof is bound to — web origin or DID.

fields.name: challenge_nonce
fields.description: Challenge nonce from the relying party for freshness.

fields.name: issued_at
fields.description: Timestamp when this proof was created.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Timestamp when this proof expires. Must be strictly after issued_at.

types.description: Binds every proof to a specific relying party, challenge, and time window. Required (never optional) for all proof generation operations. Enforces Invariant #4: all proofs are domain-bound and time-limited.

types.name: Predicate
types.description: Range predicate for Bulletproof range proofs. Defines the assertion to be proven about a committed value without revealing the value itself.

types.name: TimeBudget

fields.name: remaining_ms
fields.description: Remaining wall-clock milliseconds for proof generation. Must be positive.

fields.name: started_at_ms
fields.description: Monotonic clock timestamp (ms) when budget tracking began.

types.description: Tracks remaining wall-clock time across composed proofs. Exceeding the budget results in ProofError::TimeBudgetExceeded (fail-secure, Invariant #8). Monotonically decreasing — each proof operation deducts elapsed time.

types.name: RevealedClaims

fields.name: paths
fields.description: Ordered list of claim paths to reveal. Must be non-empty and must not include all claims in the source credential.

validators.error_message: RevealedClaims must contain at least one claim path

types.description: Newtype wrapper around a list of claim paths to selectively disclose. Prevents accidental full-credential leakage by requiring explicit construction. Maximum claim count enforced. Each ClaimPath references signet_cred::ClaimPath.

types.name: ClaimPathList
types.description: Ordered list of claim path strings referencing signet_cred::ClaimPath.

types.name: PedersenWitness

fields.name: value
fields.description: The secret committed value (u64 range). Zeroized after use.

fields.name: blinding_factor
fields.description: 32-byte scalar blinding factor. Zeroized after use. Wrapped in Zeroizing<>.

validators.error_message: Blinding factor must be exactly 32 bytes

types.description: Witness for a Pedersen commitment — the secret value and blinding factor. Both fields are Zeroizing<> in implementation. Debug impl fully redacts.

types.name: PedersenCommitment

fields.name: commitment_bytes
fields.description: Compressed Ristretto point (32 bytes) representing the commitment.

validators.error_message: Pedersen commitment must be 32 bytes (compressed Ristretto point)

types.description: A Pedersen commitment point — the public commitment to a hidden value.

types.name: SdJwtPresentation

fields.name: compact_serialization
fields.description: SD-JWT presentation in compact serialization format (tilde-delimited).

fields.name: disclosed_claim_names
fields.description: List of claim names that were disclosed in this presentation.

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: The domain binding this presentation is bound to.

types.description: An SD-JWT selective disclosure presentation in compact serialization format. Contains the issuer JWT, selected disclosure values, and key binding JWT.

types.name: BbsProof

fields.name: proof_bytes
fields.description: Serialized BBS+ proof of knowledge.

fields.name: disclosed_indices
fields.description: Indices of messages (claims) disclosed in the proof.

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: The domain binding this proof is bound to.

fields.name: embedded_nonce_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of the internally-generated nonce (for audit, not the nonce itself). 32 bytes.

types.description: A BBS+ unlinkable zero-knowledge proof. Nonce is generated internally and embedded — never caller-supplied. Two independent presentations of the same credential are computationally unlinkable.

types.name: IntList
types.description: List of integer values.

types.name: RangeProofEntry

fields.name: proof_bytes
fields.description: Serialized Bulletproof range proof.

fields.name: commitment
fields.description: The Pedersen commitment the proof is about.

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: The predicate proven (Gte, Lte, or InRange).

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: The domain binding this proof is bound to.

types.description: A Bulletproof range proof demonstrating a committed value satisfies a predicate without revealing the value.

types.name: ProofEntry
types.description: Tagged union of proof types for composition. #[non_exhaustive] to allow future proof types without breaking changes.

types.name: ProofEntryList
types.description: Ordered list of ProofEntry variants for composition.

types.name: ProofRequest

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this proof request.

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: Required domain binding for the entire request.

fields.name: requested_disclosures
fields.description: List of individual disclosure requests from the RP.

fields.name: time_budget
fields.description: Time budget for the entire proof generation pipeline.

types.description: Typestate stage 1: Incoming proof request from a relying party. Parsed and validated but not yet planned. Immutable after construction.

types.name: DisclosureRequest
types.description: A single disclosure request — SD-JWT selective disclosure, BBS+ proof, or range proof.

types.name: DisclosureRequestList
types.description: List of disclosure requests in a proof request.

types.name: SdJwtDisclosureRequest

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle referencing the cached SD-JWT credential (from signet_cred).

fields.name: claim_paths
fields.description: Claims to selectively disclose.

types.description: Request to produce an SD-JWT selective disclosure presentation.

types.name: BbsDisclosureRequest

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle referencing the cached BBS+ credential (from signet_cred).

fields.name: disclosed_indices
fields.description: Indices of messages/claims to disclose in the proof.

types.description: Request to produce a BBS+ unlinkable proof. Note: no nonce field — nonce is generated internally to enforce unlinkability.

types.name: RangeDisclosureRequest

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle referencing the credential containing the committed attribute.

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Name of the attribute to prove a range predicate about.

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: The range predicate to prove (Gte, Lte, InRange).

fields.name: witness
fields.description: The witness (value + blinding factor) for the Pedersen commitment. Zeroized after proof generation.

fields.name: commitment
fields.description: The public Pedersen commitment for this attribute.

types.description: Request to produce a Bulletproof range proof for a committed value.

types.name: ProofPlan

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Carried forward from ProofRequest.

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: Domain binding for all proofs in this plan.

fields.name: planned_entries
fields.description: Resolved and validated proof generation entries.

fields.name: remaining_budget
fields.description: Remaining time budget after planning phase.

fields.name: estimated_total_ms
fields.description: Estimated total generation time in ms based on proof types.

types.description: Typestate stage 2: A validated and resolved plan for proof generation. All credential handles have been resolved to cached credentials, all predicates validated, and the time budget has been checked for feasibility. Immutable after construction.

types.name: PlannedEntry
types.description: A resolved, validated proof generation plan entry with all inputs verified.

types.name: PlannedEntryList
types.description: List of planned proof entries.

types.name: ResolvedSdJwtPlan

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle to the cached SD-JWT credential.

fields.name: revealed_claims
fields.description: Validated claim paths to reveal.

fields.name: estimated_ms
fields.description: Estimated generation time in ms.

types.description: Resolved SD-JWT proof plan with cached credential data.

types.name: ResolvedBbsPlan

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle to the cached BBS+ credential.

fields.name: disclosed_indices
fields.description: Validated message indices to disclose.

fields.name: estimated_ms
fields.description: Estimated generation time in ms.

types.description: Resolved BBS+ proof plan with cached credential data.

types.name: ResolvedRangePlan

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle to the credential with committed attribute.

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Attribute name for the range proof.

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: Validated range predicate.

fields.name: witness
fields.description: Witness value. Zeroized after proof generation.

fields.name: commitment
fields.description: Public Pedersen commitment.

fields.name: estimated_ms
fields.description: Estimated generation time in ms.

types.description: Resolved Bulletproof range proof plan.

types.name: ProofBundle

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Carried forward from ProofPlan.

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: Domain binding for all proofs.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: Generated proof entries.

fields.name: remaining_budget
fields.description: Remaining time budget after proof generation.

types.description: Typestate stage 3: All individual proofs have been generated but not yet composed into a bound presentation. Contains heterogeneous proof entries ready for composition.

types.name: BoundPresentation

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Carried forward from original ProofRequest.

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: The domain binding this presentation is bound to.

fields.name: entries
fields.description: The composed proof entries.

fields.name: cbor_encoded
fields.description: Deterministic CBOR serialization of the composite presentation.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Timestamp when the presentation was finalized.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: Timestamp when this presentation expires (minimum of domain_binding.expires_at and all individual proof expiries).

types.description: Typestate stage 4 (terminal): A fully composed, domain-bound, time-limited presentation ready for transmission to the relying party. Serialized as deterministic CBOR. Co-produced with an AuditManifest.

types.name: AuditManifest

fields.name: request_id
fields.description: Links to the originating ProofRequest.

fields.name: rp_identifier
fields.description: Which relying party received the presentation.

fields.name: disclosed_claim_summary
fields.description: Summary of what was disclosed (claim names, proof types) — never the actual values.

fields.name: proof_types_used
fields.description: List of proof type names used (e.g., 'sd-jwt', 'bbs+', 'bulletproof').

fields.name: issued_at
fields.description: When the presentation was created.

fields.name: expires_at
fields.description: When the presentation expires.

fields.name: presentation_hash
fields.description: SHA-256 hash of the BoundPresentation CBOR bytes for integrity linking.

types.description: Audit record co-produced with every BoundPresentation. Records what was disclosed, to whom, and when. Written to the audit chain (Invariant #6: all disclosures are on the audit chain).

types.name: ClaimSummary

fields.name: credential_handle
fields.description: Handle of the credential the claim belongs to.

fields.name: claim_name
fields.description: Name/path of the disclosed claim.

fields.name: proof_type
fields.description: Type of proof used (sd-jwt, bbs+, bulletproof).

types.description: Summary of a disclosed claim for audit purposes. Contains the claim name and proof type but never the claim value.

types.name: ClaimSummaryList
types.description: List of claim disclosure summaries for audit.

types.name: StrList
types.description: List of strings.

types.name: PresentationWithAudit

fields.name: presentation
fields.description: The finalized, domain-bound presentation.

fields.name: audit_manifest
fields.description: The audit manifest to be written to the audit chain.

types.description: Composite return type co-producing a BoundPresentation with its AuditManifest. Ensures Invariant #6: no presentation can be created without a corresponding audit record.

types.name: ProofError
types.description: Error type for the signet-proof crate. #[non_exhaustive], thiserror-derived. Crypto failure variants are intentionally opaque — no inner details to prevent oracle attacks. No raw key material in any variant.

types.name: BatchRangeRequest

fields.name: entries
fields.description: Individual range proof requests to batch together. Must contain at least 2 entries (single entries should use the non-batch API).

fields.name: domain_binding
fields.description: Shared domain binding for all range proofs in the batch.

types.description: Request for batched Bulletproof range proof generation. Multiple range proofs sharing the same domain binding are generated in a single Bulletproof proving pass for efficiency.

types.name: BatchRangeEntry

fields.name: attribute_name
fields.description: Name of the attribute.

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: Range predicate to prove.

fields.name: witness
fields.description: Witness for the Pedersen commitment. Zeroized after use.

fields.name: commitment
fields.description: Public Pedersen commitment.

types.description: A single entry in a batch range proof request.

types.name: BatchRangeEntryList
types.description: List of batch range proof entries.

types.name: RangeProofEntryList
types.description: List of range proof result entries.

types.name: ProofEngineConfig

fields.name: default_ttl_seconds
fields.description: Default time-to-live for generated proofs in seconds.

validators.error_message: Default TTL must be between 10 and 3600 seconds

fields.name: minimum_remaining_ttl_seconds
fields.description: Minimum remaining TTL required on domain binding before proof generation is allowed. Prevents generating proofs that expire before delivery.

validators.error_message: Minimum remaining TTL must be between 1 and 60 seconds

fields.name: sd_jwt_budget_ms
fields.description: Per-proof time budget estimate for SD-JWT derivation in ms.

fields.name: bbs_budget_ms
fields.description: Per-proof time budget estimate for BBS+ proof generation in ms.

fields.name: range_proof_budget_ms
fields.description: Per-proof time budget estimate for Bulletproof range proof in ms.

fields.name: max_proofs_per_presentation
fields.description: Maximum number of proof entries in a single composite presentation.

validators.error_message: Max proofs per presentation must be between 1 and 64

types.description: Configuration for the ProofEngine. Immutable after construction.

functions.name: create_proof_engine
functions.description: Constructs and returns a new ProofEngine instance with the given configuration. The ProofEngine bundles SelectiveDisclosure, UnlinkableProver, RangeProver, and ProofComposer capabilities into a single entry point for the typestate pipeline.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Engine configuration. Uses defaults if not customized.

postconditions: Returned engine is ready for proof generation
Engine holds no secret material at rest

functions.name: validate_proof_request
functions.description: Typestate transition: ProofRequest → ProofPlan. Validates the incoming proof request: checks domain binding validity (not expired, minimum remaining TTL), resolves all credential handles via signet_cred, validates claim paths against credential schemas, checks predicate validity, estimates total proof generation time against the time budget, and produces an immutable ProofPlan. This is the planning/validation phase — no cryptographic operations are performed.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Engine configuration for budget estimation.

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: The incoming proof request to validate and plan.

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: DomainBinding.expires_at <= current time

error_cases.name: minimum_ttl_violated
error_cases.condition: Remaining TTL on domain binding < config.minimum_remaining_ttl_seconds

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: A credential handle cannot be resolved via signet_cred

error_cases.name: credential_expired
error_cases.condition: A referenced credential has expired

error_cases.name: credential_type_mismatch
error_cases.condition: SD-JWT request references BBS+ credential or vice versa

error_cases.name: invalid_claim_path
error_cases.condition: A claim path does not exist in the credential schema

error_cases.name: full_disclosure_prevented
error_cases.condition: RevealedClaims covers all claims in the credential (would defeat selective disclosure)

error_cases.name: invalid_predicate
error_cases.condition: Predicate is malformed (e.g., InRange where lower >= upper)

error_cases.name: time_budget_exceeded
error_cases.condition: Estimated total generation time exceeds remaining time budget

error_cases.name: invalid_domain_binding
error_cases.condition: DomainBinding fields are invalid (e.g., empty RP identifier)

postconditions: All credential handles in ProofPlan are resolvable via signet_cred
All claim paths are valid for their respective credentials
All predicates are well-formed (e.g., InRange lower < upper)
ProofPlan.estimated_total_ms <= ProofPlan.remaining_budget.remaining_ms
ProofPlan.remaining_budget.remaining_ms < request.time_budget.remaining_ms (planning time deducted)
No cryptographic operations have been performed
No secret material is held

functions.name: execute_proof_plan
functions.description: Typestate transition: ProofPlan → ProofBundle. Executes all proof generation operations described in the ProofPlan. Generates SD-JWT presentations, BBS+ proofs (with internally-generated nonces), and Bulletproof range proofs. Tracks wall-clock time and fails securely if the time budget is exceeded at any checkpoint. All witness/blinding data is zeroized after use. This is the cryptographic hot path.

inputs.name: plan
inputs.description: The validated proof plan to execute.

error_cases.name: time_budget_exceeded
error_cases.condition: Wall-clock time exceeded remaining_budget during generation

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: Domain binding expired during proof generation

error_cases.name: sd_jwt_derivation_failed
error_cases.condition: SD-JWT selective disclosure derivation failed (opaque)

error_cases.name: bbs_proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: BBS+ proof of knowledge generation failed (opaque)

error_cases.name: range_proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: Bulletproof range proof generation failed (opaque)

error_cases.name: witness_commitment_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Witness does not match the provided Pedersen commitment

error_cases.name: predicate_not_satisfied
error_cases.condition: Witness value does not satisfy the predicate (holder cannot prove a false claim)

error_cases.name: internal_crypto_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected internal cryptographic error (opaque, no details)

preconditions: plan is a valid ProofPlan (produced by validate_proof_request)
plan.domain_binding has not expired since planning
postconditions: ProofBundle contains exactly len(plan.planned_entries) proof entries
All SD-JWT presentations use compact serialization format
All BBS+ proofs used internally-generated nonces (never caller-supplied)
All range proofs verify against their commitments and predicates
All proofs are domain-bound to plan.domain_binding
All PedersenWitness blinding_factor fields have been zeroized
All BBS+ nonces have been zeroized after proof generation
ProofBundle.remaining_budget reflects actual elapsed time
If any proof generation fails, no partial ProofBundle is returned (atomic)
side_effects: Reads system monotonic clock for time budget tracking
Zeroizes secret material in memory after use

structured_side_effects.description: Zeroizes PedersenWitness blinding factors and BBS+ nonces after use
Draws random bytes for BBS+ nonce generation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of planned entries, dominated by Bulletproof range proofs at ~200ms each

functions.name: compose_presentation
functions.description: Typestate transition: ProofBundle → PresentationWithAudit (BoundPresentation + AuditManifest). Composes all proof entries in the bundle into a single BoundPresentation with deterministic CBOR serialization. Co-produces an AuditManifest recording what was disclosed (Invariant #6). Validates the final presentation has not expired (minimum-remaining-TTL check). The AuditManifest must be written to the audit chain by the caller via signet_audit before the presentation is transmitted.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Engine configuration for TTL validation.

inputs.name: bundle
inputs.description: The proof bundle to compose into a final presentation.

error_cases.name: time_budget_exceeded
error_cases.condition: Composition exceeds remaining time budget

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: Domain binding expired during composition

error_cases.name: minimum_ttl_violated
error_cases.condition: Resulting presentation would have less than minimum_remaining_ttl_seconds before expiry

error_cases.name: cbor_serialization_failed
error_cases.condition: Deterministic CBOR encoding failed

error_cases.name: composition_failed
error_cases.condition: Proof entry composition failed (e.g., conflicting domain bindings)

preconditions: bundle is a valid ProofBundle (produced by execute_proof_plan)
postconditions: BoundPresentation.cbor_encoded is deterministic CBOR of the composite presentation
BoundPresentation.expires_at <= bundle.domain_binding.expires_at
BoundPresentation.expires_at is the minimum expiry across all proof entries and domain binding
AuditManifest.presentation_hash == SHA-256(BoundPresentation.cbor_encoded)
AuditManifest.disclosed_claim_summary lists every disclosed claim (no omissions)
AuditManifest contains no actual claim values (only names and types)
AuditManifest.rp_identifier matches bundle.domain_binding.rp_identifier
SD-JWT entries retain compact serialization within the CBOR envelope
No secret material is present in the BoundPresentation or AuditManifest
side_effects: Reads system clock for timestamp generation
Computes SHA-256 hash of CBOR-encoded presentation

structured_side_effects.description: Pure computation — audit manifest is returned, not written (caller writes via signet_audit)

functions.name: derive_sd_jwt_presentation
functions.description: SelectiveDisclosure trait: Derives an SD-JWT selective disclosure presentation from a cached SD-JWT credential. Selects the specified claims for disclosure and produces a key-binding JWT bound to the domain. Uses sd-jwt-rust (OpenWallet Foundation). This is the standalone SD-JWT derivation for use outside the typestate pipeline.

inputs.name: credential_handle
inputs.description: Handle referencing the cached SD-JWT credential from signet_cred.

inputs.name: revealed_claims
inputs.description: Claims to selectively disclose. Must be a proper subset of the credential's claims.

inputs.name: domain_binding
inputs.description: Required domain binding for the presentation.

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: credential_handle does not resolve

error_cases.name: credential_expired
error_cases.condition: Referenced credential has expired

error_cases.name: credential_type_mismatch
error_cases.condition: credential_handle references a non-SD-JWT credential

error_cases.name: invalid_claim_path
error_cases.condition: A claim path does not exist in the credential

error_cases.name: claim_path_not_selective
error_cases.condition: A claim path references a non-selectively-disclosable claim

error_cases.name: full_disclosure_prevented
error_cases.condition: All claims would be disclosed

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: DomainBinding has expired

error_cases.name: sd_jwt_derivation_failed
error_cases.condition: Internal SD-JWT derivation error (opaque)

preconditions: credential_handle resolves to a valid, non-expired SD-JWT credential
revealed_claims.paths are valid claim paths in the credential
revealed_claims does not cover all claims (full disclosure prevented)
postconditions: Returned SdJwtPresentation.compact_serialization is valid SD-JWT compact format
Only the specified claims are disclosed
Key binding JWT is bound to domain_binding.rp_identifier and domain_binding.challenge_nonce
Presentation verifies against the issuer's public key

performance_budget.complexity: O(c) where c = number of claims in credential

functions.name: generate_bbs_proof
functions.description: UnlinkableProver trait: Generates a BBS+ unlinkable zero-knowledge proof from a cached BBS+ credential. The nonce is generated internally (never caller-supplied) to enforce unlinkability — this is a critical security property. Two calls with the same inputs produce unlinkable proofs. Uses anoncreds-v2-rs.

inputs.name: credential_handle
inputs.description: Handle referencing the cached BBS+ credential from signet_cred.

inputs.name: disclosed_indices
inputs.description: Indices of messages/claims to disclose in the proof. Remaining messages are hidden.

inputs.name: domain_binding
inputs.description: Required domain binding for the proof.

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: credential_handle does not resolve

error_cases.name: credential_expired
error_cases.condition: Referenced credential has expired

error_cases.name: credential_type_mismatch
error_cases.condition: credential_handle references a non-BBS+ credential

error_cases.name: full_disclosure_prevented
error_cases.condition: All messages would be disclosed

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: DomainBinding has expired

error_cases.name: bbs_proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: Internal BBS+ proof generation error (opaque — no oracle)

preconditions: credential_handle resolves to a valid, non-expired BBS+ credential
disclosed_indices are valid indices within the credential's message count
disclosed_indices is a proper subset (not all messages disclosed)
postconditions: Returned BbsProof verifies against the issuer's BBS+ public key
Only messages at disclosed_indices are revealed
Proof uses an internally-generated 32-byte random nonce
BbsProof.embedded_nonce_hash = SHA-256(internally_generated_nonce)
Internal nonce has been zeroized after proof generation
Proof is domain-bound to domain_binding.rp_identifier and domain_binding.challenge_nonce
Two sequential calls with identical inputs produce computationally unlinkable proofs (statistical property)
side_effects: Generates 32 bytes of cryptographic randomness for internal nonce
Zeroizes internal nonce after proof generation

structured_side_effects.description: Draws 32 random bytes for internal nonce generation
Zeroizes internal nonce after embedding in proof

performance_budget.complexity: O(m) where m = number of messages in credential

functions.name: generate_range_proof
functions.description: RangeProver trait: Generates a Bulletproof range proof from a Pedersen commitment witness, proving the committed value satisfies the given predicate without revealing the value. Uses dalek bulletproofs (nightly). Witness is zeroized after proof generation.

inputs.name: witness
inputs.description: The secret value and blinding factor for the Pedersen commitment. Zeroized after use.

inputs.name: commitment
inputs.description: The public Pedersen commitment to prove about.

inputs.name: predicate
inputs.description: The range predicate to prove (Gte, Lte, InRange).

inputs.name: domain_binding
inputs.description: Required domain binding for the proof.

error_cases.name: witness_commitment_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Witness does not correspond to the given commitment

error_cases.name: predicate_not_satisfied
error_cases.condition: Witness value does not satisfy the predicate

error_cases.name: invalid_predicate
error_cases.condition: Predicate is malformed (e.g., InRange lower >= upper)

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: DomainBinding has expired

error_cases.name: range_proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: Internal Bulletproof range proof generation error (opaque)

preconditions: witness.value satisfies predicate (cannot prove a false statement)
witness matches commitment (re-computing commitment from witness yields commitment)
predicate is well-formed (InRange: lower < upper; Gte/Lte: value in u64 range)
postconditions: Returned RangeProofEntry.proof_bytes verifies against commitment and predicate
Proof is domain-bound to domain_binding
witness.blinding_factor has been zeroized
witness.value has been zeroized
No witness material appears in the returned proof or error
side_effects: Zeroizes witness material after proof generation

structured_side_effects.description: Zeroizes PedersenWitness value and blinding_factor after proof generation

performance_budget.complexity: O(log n) where n = range size in bits (64-bit Bulletproof)

functions.name: batch_range_prove
functions.description: RangeProver trait (batch): Generates multiple Bulletproof range proofs in a single proving pass for efficiency. All proofs share the same domain binding. Uses dalek bulletproofs aggregated proving. More efficient than individual proofs when 2+ range proofs are needed. All witnesses are zeroized after proof generation.

inputs.name: batch_request
inputs.description: Batch of range proof requests sharing a domain binding. Must contain >= 2 entries.

error_cases.name: too_few_entries
error_cases.condition: batch_request.entries.len() < 2

error_cases.name: witness_commitment_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Any witness does not match its commitment

error_cases.name: predicate_not_satisfied
error_cases.condition: Any witness does not satisfy its predicate

error_cases.name: invalid_predicate
error_cases.condition: Any predicate is malformed

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: Domain binding has expired

error_cases.name: batch_range_proof_failed
error_cases.condition: Internal batch Bulletproof generation error (opaque)

preconditions: All witnesses satisfy their respective predicates
All witnesses match their respective commitments
postconditions: Returned list has same length as batch_request.entries
Each RangeProofEntry independently verifies against its commitment and predicate
All proofs are domain-bound to batch_request.domain_binding
All witness blinding factors and values have been zeroized
Total generation time < N * 200ms (batch efficiency)
side_effects: Zeroizes all witness material after proof generation

structured_side_effects.description: Zeroizes all PedersenWitness values and blinding_factors after batch proof generation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n * log b) where n = number of proofs, b = range size in bits (aggregated Bulletproof)

functions.name: run_proof_pipeline
functions.description: ProofEngine convenience method: Runs the full typestate pipeline ProofRequest → ProofPlan → ProofBundle → PresentationWithAudit in a single call. Internally calls validate_proof_request, execute_proof_plan, and compose_presentation in sequence. Fails securely at any stage. This is the primary entry point for most callers.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Engine configuration.

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: The proof request to process.

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: DomainBinding expired at any pipeline stage

error_cases.name: minimum_ttl_violated
error_cases.condition: Remaining TTL below minimum at any stage

error_cases.name: credential_not_found
error_cases.condition: Any credential handle cannot be resolved

error_cases.name: credential_expired
error_cases.condition: Any referenced credential has expired

error_cases.name: credential_type_mismatch
error_cases.condition: Credential type does not match request type

error_cases.name: invalid_claim_path
error_cases.condition: A claim path does not exist in the credential

error_cases.name: full_disclosure_prevented
error_cases.condition: Full disclosure attempted

error_cases.name: invalid_predicate
error_cases.condition: A predicate is malformed

error_cases.name: predicate_not_satisfied
error_cases.condition: A witness does not satisfy its predicate

error_cases.name: time_budget_exceeded
error_cases.condition: Time budget exceeded at any stage (fail-secure)

error_cases.name: witness_commitment_mismatch
error_cases.condition: A witness does not match its commitment

error_cases.name: sd_jwt_derivation_failed
error_cases.condition: SD-JWT derivation failed (opaque)

error_cases.name: bbs_proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: BBS+ proof generation failed (opaque)

error_cases.name: range_proof_generation_failed
error_cases.condition: Range proof generation failed (opaque)

error_cases.name: cbor_serialization_failed
error_cases.condition: CBOR serialization failed

error_cases.name: composition_failed
error_cases.condition: Proof composition failed

error_cases.name: internal_crypto_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected cryptographic error (opaque)

postconditions: All postconditions of validate_proof_request, execute_proof_plan, and compose_presentation apply
AuditManifest is co-produced (caller must write to audit chain before transmitting presentation)
All secret material has been zeroized
If any stage fails, no partial results are returned (atomic pipeline)
side_effects: All side effects of validate_proof_request, execute_proof_plan, and compose_presentation

structured_side_effects.description: Zeroizes all secret material after pipeline completion
Draws random bytes for BBS+ nonce generation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n = number of proof entries, dominated by Bulletproof range proofs

functions.name: validate_domain_binding
functions.description: Validates a DomainBinding for correctness and liveness. Checks that expires_at > issued_at, that the binding has not expired, that the remaining TTL meets the minimum threshold, and that the RP identifier is well-formed. Utility function used internally by all proof generation functions.

inputs.name: domain_binding
inputs.description: The domain binding to validate.

inputs.name: minimum_remaining_ttl_seconds
inputs.description: Minimum remaining TTL required in seconds.

error_cases.name: domain_binding_expired
error_cases.condition: expires_at <= current time

error_cases.name: minimum_ttl_violated
error_cases.condition: Remaining TTL < minimum_remaining_ttl_seconds

error_cases.name: invalid_domain_binding
error_cases.condition: expires_at <= issued_at, or RP identifier is empty/malformed

postconditions: Returns true only if all validation checks pass
No side effects

functions.name: serialize_presentation_cbor
functions.description: Serializes a BoundPresentation to deterministic CBOR using ciborium. Produces canonical byte ordering for reproducible hashing. SD-JWT entries within the CBOR retain their compact serialization as string values.

inputs.name: presentation
inputs.description: The presentation to serialize.

error_cases.name: cbor_serialization_failed
error_cases.condition: CBOR encoding failed

postconditions: Output is valid deterministic CBOR
Deserializing the output produces an equivalent BoundPresentation
SD-JWT entries are represented as CBOR text strings in compact serialization
Output is reproducible (same input always produces identical bytes)

functions.name: deserialize_presentation_cbor
functions.description: Deserializes a BoundPresentation from CBOR bytes. Validates structural integrity but does not verify cryptographic proofs (verification is the relying party's responsibility).

inputs.name: cbor_bytes
inputs.description: CBOR-encoded presentation bytes.

error_cases.name: cbor_deserialization_failed
error_cases.condition: Input is not valid CBOR or does not match BoundPresentation schema

postconditions: Returned BoundPresentation is structurally valid
Cryptographic proofs are NOT verified (structural deserialization only)
Re-serializing produces bytes identical to input (round-trip)

invariants: INV-01: All proofs are domain-bound — no proof generation function can be called without a valid, non-expired DomainBinding. DomainBinding is a required parameter (never optional) on every proof generation path.
INV-02: All proofs are time-limited — every proof has an explicit expires_at and the pipeline checks remaining TTL at every stage. Pipeline timeout = deny (fail-secure).

INV-03: BBS+ nonces are always generated internally — the UnlinkableProver trait signature has no nonce input parameter. Two presentations of the same credential are computationally unlinkable. Statistically tested with N≥1000 proofs using chi-squared and K-S tests.
INV-04: No full credential disclosure — RevealedClaims newtype and disclosed_indices validation prevent disclosing all claims/messages. FullDisclosurePrevented error enforced at validation time.

INV-05: All secret material is zeroized after use — PedersenWitness blinding_factor and value, BBS+ internal nonces, and any intermediate secret scalars are wrapped in Zeroizing<> and zeroized on drop.

INV-06: Every BoundPresentation co-produces an AuditManifest — PresentationWithAudit return type makes it impossible to obtain a presentation without an audit record. Caller must write AuditManifest to signet_audit before transmitting.

INV-07: Crypto failure errors are opaque — SdJwtDerivationFailed, BbsProofGenerationFailed, RangeProofGenerationFailed, and InternalCryptoError carry no inner details. No raw key material in any error variant. Prevents oracle attacks.

INV-08: Typestate pipeline prevents misuse — ProofRequest, ProofPlan, ProofBundle, and BoundPresentation are distinct types with no public constructors. Each can only be produced by the preceding pipeline stage.
INV-09: No algorithm negotiation — proof types and algorithms are fixed at compile time. No runtime algorithm selection or negotiation in any protocol path.
INV-10: One-way data flow — proofs flow vault → agent → service (RP). The proof engine never accepts or processes data flowing from service back toward the vault.

INV-11: Debug impls redact all secret material — PedersenWitness, Nonce, and any type containing secrets implement Debug with redacted output (e.g., 'PedersenWitness { value: [REDACTED], blinding_factor: [REDACTED] }').
INV-12: Atomic pipeline — if any stage of the typestate pipeline fails, no partial results are returned. All-or-nothing proof generation.
INV-13: Deterministic CBOR serialization — same BoundPresentation always serializes to identical bytes via ciborium deterministic encoding. Required for reproducible AuditManifest.presentation_hash.
INV-14: Tier 3 compartment keys never held by agent — proof engine operates on cached credentials and derived proofs only. No Tier 3 vault decryption keys are accessible to the proof engine.

INV-15: Witness-commitment consistency checked before proof generation — generate_range_proof and batch_range_prove verify that each witness matches its commitment before attempting proof generation. WitnessCommitmentMismatch returned on mismatch.
requires: signet_cred: provides CachedCredential, CredentialHandle, ClaimPath, IssuerPublicKey, BbsPublicKey, SdJwtCredential, BbsCredential
signet_vault: provides VaultSealedKey and KeyDerivationContext for compartmentalized key access
signet_audit: provides AuditChainWriter for recording all disclosures on the audit chain (Invariant #6)
aws-lc-rs: FIPS-path primitives for HKDF-SHA-256 domain binding derivation
anoncreds-v2-rs: BBS+ signature and proof primitives
dalek bulletproofs (nightly): Pedersen commitments and range proof generation
sd-jwt-rust (OpenWallet Foundation): SD-JWT selective disclosure presentation
ciborium: deterministic CBOR serialization for composite presentations
rusty_paseto: PASETO v4 capability token validation for agent session verification
zeroize: Zeroizing<> wrapper for secret material
